channel.title: 🎯 Gameplay Guide on 🎮 Human: Fall Flat - Walkthroughs & Guides
channel.description: Recent content in 🎯 Gameplay Guide on 🎮 Human: Fall Flat - Walkthroughs & Guides

item.title: Basic Controls Guide
item.description:
🎮 Basic Controls Guide

In Human: Fall Flat, Bob’s wobbly body has two independently controlled arms. Every keyboard/mouse input is converted into torque output for his wobbly muscles. This guide will help you build muscle memory and master the fundamental movement physics.

📸 Basic Climb: Grab the ledge with both hands, look down, and pull yourself up.

⌨️ Input Command Terminal

Familiarize yourself with the default key bindings to coordinate Bob’s limbs:

  • Move Walk: Use WASD.
  • Rotate Head/View: Move your Mouse. Bob’s hands reach toward the center of your crosshair.
  • Left Grab: Hold Left Mouse Button. Left hand sticks to the target.
  • Right Grab: Hold Right Mouse Button. Right hand sticks to the target.
  • Jump: Press Space. Jumping carries forward momentum when moving.
  • Run: Hold Shift. Bob bends over slightly and accelerates.

📐 Mechanics & Core Movement Systems

1. Grab & Release System

  • The Physics: As long as you hold down the Left/Right Click, Bob’s hands act as high-strength sticky pads. Any surface (walls, boxes, teammates) will be stuck tight.
  • Tip: Release the mouse buttons to let go. When carrying objects, keep the hands flat and pull backwards to slide items smoothly.

2. Standard Wall Climb (Step-by-Step)

This is the most critical hurdle for new players. Master this 4-step sequence:

  1. Approach & Look Up: Move up to the wall, look straight up at the sky, and raise both hands high.
  2. Jump & Grab: Press Space to jump. Ensure both hands stick to the top ledge of the wall. Keep holding the mouse buttons!
  3. Pull Up (Look Down): Slowly move your mouse down to look at the ground. Bob will naturally pull his torso up to the ledge.
  4. Forward Over: While looking down, press and hold W. Bob will swing his legs over and climb on top.
【Climbing Tip for Beginners】
Many beginners fail because they release the mouse buttons too early when trying to step onto the ledge. Remember: Never release your mouse clicks until both feet are firmly on top of the wall! Keep looking down until Bob climbs over.

For advanced physics engines techniques, check out our Core Physics Tricks Guide.

item.title: Core Physics Tricks Guide
item.description:
💪 Core Physics Tricks Guide

Once you master basic grabs, you can unlock Bob’s inertia engine. By combining coordinate rotations and friction limits, you can perform advanced movements that bypass standard puzzles entirely.

📸 Swing Jump: Converting gravitational potential energy into forward speed.

🎛️ Training Modules: Advanced Physics Guides

1. The Swing Jump (Pendulum Action)

  • Difficulty: ⭐⭐☆☆☆
  • Application: Crossing super-wide chasms using ropes, poles, or hanging lanterns.
  • Action Sequence:
    1. Jump and grab the rope/lantern at the highest possible point.
    2. Hold W and S in cycles. Push forward at the peak of the backward swing, and pull back at the peak of the forward swing to build momentum.
    3. Once the swing reaches maximum speed, wait until you reach the highest point of the forward arc (when speed drops to zero for an instant).
    4. Release both grabs instantly, and press Space to launch yourself forward.

2. Single-Hand Wall Climb (Horizontal Wiggle)

  • Difficulty: ⭐⭐⭐⭐☆
  • Application: Climbing flat vertical walls without any ledges.
  • Action Sequence:
    1. Face the wall, raise one hand (e.g. left hand) and grab it. Leave the other hand free.
    2. Swing your camera to the right, raising the free right hand above the left hand. Grab the wall with your right hand.
    3. Release the left hand click. Swing your camera to the left, raising the left hand above the right hand, and grab.
    4. Repeat this coordinate rotation back and forth to walk your hands up the wall.

🏆 Physics Training Matrix

Trick Name Difficulty Core Mechanism Training Map Recommendation
Swing Jump ⭐⭐☆☆☆ Gravity & Pendulum Swing Mountain, Castle
Glitch Throwing ⭐⭐⭐☆☆ Inertia & Torque Release Carry, Factory
Single-Hand Climb ⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ Coordinate Wiggling & Friction Mansion, Mountain
Super Jump ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Frame-Rate Collision Glitch Any flat ground
💡 Tip for Glitch Climb: Always make sure your camera is rotated at least 45 degrees sideways when raising the free hand. This forces Bob's shoulder joints to stretch higher than normal, allowing you to reach further up the wall.

Ready to test your training? Head over to our Level Guides Index to check all 30 official maps!
